credit-card.notes: Florida reduced its statute of limitations for written contracts from 5 years to 5 years (note: in 2023 HB 837 changed many SOL periods — credit card agreements signed before July 1, 2023 may have a 5-year SOL; agreements after may have 4 years for breach of written contract). Verify the current law for your specific situation.
credit-card.citation: Fla. Stat. § 95.11(2)(b) (pre-2023); Fla. Stat. § 95.11(3)(p) (post-HB 837 for some claims)

medical.notes: Medical malpractice claims have a 2-year SOL, but medical billing debt (breach of contract for services) is generally 4-5 years. Florida also enacted medical debt protections in 2023 limiting credit reporting of medical debt.

auto-loan.notes: Auto loan deficiency balances are subject to Florida's 5-year limitation for written contracts from the date of the deficiency after vehicle sale.

personal-loan.notes: Personal loans in Florida are subject to the 5-year limitation period for written contracts.

student-loan.notes: Private student loans in Florida are subject to the 5-year written contract limitation. Federal loans are not subject to state SOL for government collection.

mortgage-deficiency.notes: Florida has a strict 1-year limitation on deficiency judgments after foreclosure, making Florida one of the strongest anti-deficiency states. After 1 year from the certificate of title, no deficiency can be sought.

utility-bill.notes: Utility bill debts in Florida are typically treated as open accounts subject to a 4-year limitation period.

regulatoryBody: Florida Office of Financial Regulation (OFR) & Florida Attorney General
complaints: File a complaint with Florida OFR at flofr.gov or the FL AG at myfloridalegal.com. Florida's Consumer Collection Practices Act (Fla. Stat. § 559.55 et seq.) supplements the FDCPA.
